_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
Amendment C376 is a property development planning regulation, not a freight operations regulation. Its operational system requirements apply directly to developers and building permit applicants. For the workspace, operational system implications are indirect but materially relevant across the following areas:

**1. Property and Facilities Intelligence Integration**
Scope: Any Melbourne office, warehouse, staging area, or event logistics hub the workspace occupies or evaluates for occupation.
Requirement: Add C376 compliance status as a mandatory field in facilities due diligence workflows. When evaluating new Melbourne tenancies, confirm whether the proposed building is C376-compliant (for new builds post-gazettal) or whether any planned landlord extensions will trigger compliance. C376-compliant buildings will carry lower operational running costs for heating, cooling, and water — relevant to total occupancy cost modelling.
Deadline: Implement at next facilities review cycle or immediately for any active Melbourne site search.
Gap from baseline: Most standard lease due diligence checklists do not yet include ESD planning compliance fields.

**2. Client Development Project Support**
Scope: Workspace clients in live events venue construction, luxury retail fit-out, high-value automotive showroom development, film studio builds, or humanitarian facility construction within Melbourne municipality.
Requirement: Build a C376 compliance advisory checklist into the workspace's client onboarding and project initiation procedures for Melbourne-based capital works. Confirm permit lodgement timing relative to gazettal. Ensure clients have engaged a suitably qualified ESD consultant before lodging any planning permit for an in-scope project.
Deadline: Implement prior to gazettal (monitoring required).
Gap from baseline: Client development project checklists are unlikely to currently include Victorian-specific ESD planning compliance triggers.

**3. Supplier and Venue Contracting**
Scope: Contracts with Melbourne venue operators, bonded storage providers, and fitout contractors for in-scope projects.
Requirement: Insert C376 status representations and warranty clauses in venue and facility agreements where the workspace is responsible for selecting or recommending a facility that will be newly constructed post-gazettal. Ensure construction and fitout subcontractors used by workspace clients in Melbourne are aware of DDO73 mandatory standards and Green Star, BESS, and NatHERS compliance obligations that must be "back-to-backed" into construction contracts.
Deadline: At next contract renewal or new contract execution for Melbourne facilities.
Gap from baseline: Standard freight and logistics facility contracts do not currently reference DDO73 or ESD planning scheme requirements.

**4. ESD Tooling Awareness (No In-House Build Required)**
No internal tracking system build is mandated for the workspace as a freight forwarder. However, awareness of the four primary compliance tools — BESS, Green Star Buildings, NatHERS, and the City of Melbourne Green Factor Tool (free to use) — is required to advise clients effectively. The Green Factor Tool is publicly available and free; BESS is the only dedicated Victorian planning-stage sustainability assessment tool.

*Source: How does this affect me? | Amendment C376, Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376/how-does-this-affect-me; Gadens, Greening Melbourne: Amendment C376 Melbourne Planning Scheme, April 2023. https://www.gadens.com/legal-insights/greening-melbourne-amendment-c376-melbourne-planning-scheme-sustainable-building-design-amendment/*
